stage: v0.3 P2.9 Benchmark — KB-5 Gap Closure
decision: Use MCP search tools (mcp_paper-search + mcp_english-search) to close the KB-5 domain knowledge gap. KB-5 (AfterValidator annotation propagation through FastAPI dependency analysis) was the only remaining false negative across 3 benchmark projects. The root cause was identified via MCP search: FastAPI PR #13442 documented that PR #13314's Pydantic 2.11 compat change inadvertently stripped Annotated metadata (AfterValidator, BeforeValidator) from the dependency analysis chain. This knowledge was synthesized into a reusable reference file (references/python-pydantic-audit-rules.md) with 3 detection rules, following the security-best-practices skill's references/ directory pattern. The reference file can be loaded by v0.5 P3.1 guards.yml system.
rationale: KB-5 was the sole remaining false negative (1/13 unknown bugs missed, 92.3% detection rate). MCP search tools provided access to the actual GitHub PRs and Pydantic API documentation that generic web search could not surface with sufficient depth. The search confirmed: (1) PR #13314 broke Annotated metadata propagation, (2) PR #13442 reverted + added tests, (3) the detection pattern involves checking typing.get_args(annotated_type) processing in dependencies/utils.py and _compat.py. Writing this as a reference file rather than hardcoding into SKILL.md keeps the architecture pluggable (v0.5 P3.1 guards.yml pattern). The MCP search pattern — find PR → read docs → synthesize detection rules → persist to reference file — is now validated as a repeatable knowledge-gap-closure workflow.
next_action: Proceed to v0.4: Implement P2.11 Iterative Audit + P2.13 Repair Guidance + P2.10 Deterministic Assist Layer. The KB-5 reference file will be loaded when v0.5 P3.1 guards.yml system is implemented.
